children.heading: Compliance with chapter 83 of title 41
children.label: a Compliance with chapter 83 of title 41
children.text: No funds authorized to be appropriated under this Act or any amendment made by this Act may be expended by an entity unless the entity, in expending the funds, complies with chapter 83 of title 41.

children.heading: Debarment of persons convicted of fraudulent use of “Made in America” labels
children.label: b Debarment of persons convicted of fraudulent use of “Made in America” labels

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency determines that a person has been convicted of intentionally affixing a label bearing a “Made in America” inscription to any product sold in or shipped to the United States that is not made in America, the Administrator shall determine, not later than 90 days after determining that the person has been so convicted, whether the person should be debarred from contracting under the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 5121

children.heading: Definition of debar
children.label: 2 Definition of debar
children.text: In this subsection, the term “debar” has the meaning given the term in .section 4654(c) of title 10
